regions.us-ports: Today, I'm monitoring a relatively calm situation in Miami, with current conditions showing a gentle northeast wind of 9kt and clear skies, accompanied by a 16% rain probability. Over the next 24-48 hours, I expect the thunderstorms in Miami to give way to rain showers in Port Everglades and Port Canaveral, with a 16% rain probability on the second day, which may affect embarkation and disembarkation procedures. Beyond 48 hours, a 33% rain probability and drizzle are expected in the region, which could impact cruise ship operations in Tampa Bay. As the situation continues to evolve, I will be keeping a close eye on the developing weather patterns to ensure the safety of cruise passengers and crew.
